skill.name: SEO Fundamentals
skill.description: Core principles of SEO including E-E-A-T, Core Web Vitals, technical foundations, content quality, and how modern search engines evaluate pages.
skill.summary: Learn why SEO works through foundational principles of search engine quality evaluation, not tactical shortcuts.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 7 instances of 'weak cryptographic algorithm' patterns, but manual review confirms these are FALSE POSITIVES. The flagged lines (7, 113-116) contain simple string matching for HTML meta description tags, not cryptographic operations. The seo_checker.py script only reads local files and outputs analysis results with no network access, external command execution, or code execution capabilities. This is a safe educational skill about SEO principles.

content.user_title: Master SEO Fundamentals for Search Visibility
content.value_statement: Many struggle with SEO because they focus on tactics instead of principles. This skill explains how search engines evaluate quality, enabling sustainable search visibility through E-E-A-T, Core Web Vitals, and content excellence.
actual_capabilities: Explain the E-E-A-T framework and how search engines evaluate content quality
Describe Core Web Vitals metrics and their impact on page experience
Identify technical SEO requirements for crawlability and indexing
Guide content optimization for relevance and user intent
Explain structured data and schema markup purposes
Provide principles for AI-assisted content creation
limitations: Does not execute specific SEO tools or automated audits
Does not provide real-time search ranking data or analytics
Does not replace dedicated SEO platforms like Ahrefs or SEMrush
Focuses on principles rather than step-by-step tactical guides

use_cases.title: Content Strategist Learning SEO
use_cases.description: A content strategist needs to understand why certain pages rank higher than others to guide their content creation process effectively.

use_cases.title: Developer Building SEO-Aware Components
use_cases.description: A frontend developer wants to understand SEO principles to build React components that follow best practices for search visibility.

use_cases.title: Business Owner Evaluating SEO Advice
use_cases.description: A small business owner wants to understand SEO fundamentals to evaluate recommendations from agencies and avoid harmful shortcuts.

prompt_templates.title: Basic E-E-A-T Explanation
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain what E-E-A-T means in SEO and why Google uses this framework to evaluate content quality.

prompt_templates.title: Core Web Vitals Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: My website has an LCP of 3.2 seconds and CLS of 0.15. Explain what these Core Web Vitals mean and whether they meet Google's recommended thresholds.

prompt_templates.title: Technical SEO Audit Guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: I'm launching a new e-commerce site. What technical SEO elements should I verify before going live to ensure search engines can crawl and index my pages properly?

prompt_templates.title: AI Content Quality Review
prompt_templates.prompt: I drafted a blog post using AI assistance. Review these principles and tell me what I should verify before publishing to ensure the content meets quality standards for search visibility.

output_examples.input: User asks about the difference between E-E-A-T and traditional ranking factors
output_examples.output: E-E-A-T is a quality evaluation framework, not a direct ranking factor. It stands for Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, and Trustworthiness. Search engines use this framework to assess content quality, especially for YMYL topics. Experience shows first-hand involvement, Expertise demonstrates subject knowledge, Authoritativeness reflects industry recognition, and Trustworthiness indicates reliability and safety.

output_examples.input: User asks if Core Web Vitals can make or break their rankings
output_examples.output: Core Web Vitals rarely override poor content quality. They function as a tiebreaker between comparable pages. A fast page with thin content will not outrank a comprehensive resource with slower load times. However, failing Core Web Vitals can hold back otherwise strong pages. Focus first on content quality, then optimize page experience for competitive advantage.

best_practices: Prioritize content relevance and quality over technical optimization tricks
Build trust signals through transparency, accuracy, and consistent quality over time
Use structured data to help search engines understand content meaning and context
anti_patterns: Publishing unedited AI-generated content without human review and fact-checking
Focusing on keyword density instead of answering user intent comprehensively
Chasing quick ranking gains through manipulative link building or thin content

faq.question: Is E-E-A-T a direct ranking factor?
faq.answer: No, E-E-A-T is not a direct ranking factor. It is a framework used by search engines to evaluate content quality. The signals associated with each dimension may influence rankings indirectly through how content is assessed relative to competing pages.

faq.question: Do Core Web Vitals affect rankings significantly?
faq.answer: Core Web Vitals have a minor ranking impact compared to content quality and relevance. They function primarily as a tiebreaker between similar pages and can hold back otherwise strong content if they fail significantly.

faq.question: Can AI-generated content rank well in search engines?
faq.answer: Yes, search engines evaluate output quality, not authorship method. AI-assisted content can rank well if it is accurate, original, useful, and reviewed by humans for quality. The risk comes from publishing unedited AI output without verification.

faq.question: What is the most important SEO factor?
faq.answer: Content relevance and quality that satisfies user intent is the highest-weighted factor. Technical SEO enables ranking by making pages accessible, but content quality earns ranking by providing value to users.

faq.question: How long does it take to see SEO results?
faq.answer: SEO results depend on competition, content quality, and existing authority. New pages may be indexed within days but can take weeks or months to rank competitively. Trust signals accumulate over time, making SEO a long-term strategy.

faq.question: Do I need structured data for my pages to rank?
faq.answer: No, structured data is not required for ranking. It helps search engines understand content meaning and can enable eligibility for rich results, but pages can rank well without schema markup if content quality is strong.
